feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Fiori
feed.subtitle: SAP Fiori Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: RAP‑Based Service Does Not Support Aggregation Totals with Filters and Group‑By with Crud
entry.content:
Problem Description:

We are implementing a Fiori Elements List Report using a RAP‑based OData service (OData V2 or V4).
The business requirement is to show aggregated totals (SUM) for multiple numeric columns. These totals must update dynamically whenever:

  • Filters are applied
  • Group By is enabled
  • Group levels expand/collapse
  • Sorting or table personalization is applied

Most Important Requirement:

When the List Report table is grouped, the system must display an aggregation subtotal row for each group (group-level total), not only a grand total.

However, RAP currently does not allow analytical annotations or analytical CDS entities to be used with transactional behavior. Because of this limitation:

  • We cannot enable annotations such as
    • @analytics.query: true
    • @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
    • @Aggregation.default: #SUM
  • RAP services cannot perform backend aggregations or expose group-level totals.
  • As a result, neither grand totals nor group-level totals can appear in the List Report.

Observed Limitation:

Backend (RAP):

  • RAP business objects with behavior definitions do not support analytical CDS views.
  • Aggregations and group-by queries are not supported in RAP OData V2 or OData V4.
  • Analytical annotations cannot be used on projection or interface views participating in RAP.

entry.title: RAP‑Based Service Does Not Support Aggregation Totals with Filters and Group‑By
entry.content:
We are implementing a Fiori Elements List Report using a RAP‑based OData service (OData V2 or V4).
The business requirement is to show aggregated totals (SUM) for multiple numeric columns. These totals must update dynamically whenever:

  • Filters are applied
  • Group By is enabled
  • Group levels expand/collapse
  • Sorting or table personalization is applied

    Most Important Requirement:

    When the List Report table is grouped, the system must display an aggregation subtotal row for each group (group-level total), not only a grand total.

    However, RAP currently does not allow analytical annotations or analytical CDS entities to be used with transactional behavior. Because of this limitation:

    • We cannot enable annotations such as
      • @analytical.query: true
      • @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      • @Aggregation.default: #SUM
      • RAP services cannot perform backend aggregations or expose group-level totals.
      • As a result, neither grand totals nor group-level totals can appear in the List Report.

entry.title: Adapt UI changes mistakenly done in Test client (210) instead of Dev (200) – how to properly revert
entry.content:
We follow a standard landscape approach where client 200 is used for development/configuration and client 210 is used only for unit testing.

Originally, all development and configuration were done in 200, and the changes were moved to 210 for testing. After testing, a new requirement came to hide/edit some fields in a Fiori app.

While searching, I found the Key User role (SAP_UI_FLEX_KEY_USER) for Adapt UI, assigned it to a test user, and performed Adapt UI changes directly in client 210. I also activated those changes in 210.

Later, I realized this was incorrect, as Adapt UI changes should be created only in the development client (200) and then moved to 210 for testing.

To revert the mistake, I:

  • Opened Adapt UI in 210
  • Switched back to Original App and activated it

However:

  • There is no option to delete or discard Adapt UI versions
  • Older versions still exist (inactive)
  • I understand SAP does not support deleting UI Adaptation versions, only activating another version

My questions are:

  1. Is activating the Original App the correct and only supported way to revert mistaken Adapt UI changes done in 210?
  2. When I now create Adapt UI changes correctly in 200 and move them to 210 for testing, how can I ensure there is no conflict with the earlier versions created in 210?
  3. Are inactive Adapt UI versions (created earlier in 210) completely ignored once a new version from 200 is activated/published?
  4. What is the recommended best practice to avoid such conflicts when working across multiple clients (Dev → Test → Quality)?

Any guidance or SAP‑recommended approach would be appreciated.

entry.title: OData V4 URL Encoding Issue with SAP Web Dispatcher + Proxy (Sales Order Management App)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are facing an issue with OData V4 URL encoding after activating the standard Sales Order Management application in S/4HANA.

Note: An SAP incident has already been created, and in parallel we are reaching out to the community to learn from experts who may have faced a similar issue.

Issue summary

The problem occurs because equal signs (=) in the OData V4 request URL are being URL-encoded (%3D) before the request reaches the S/4HANA ICM.

In the browser, the request URL is correct and the = signs are not encoded:

https://etgwdsp.etgworld.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/c_salesordermanage_srv/srvd_f4/sap/c_orgdivisionvaluehelp/0001;ps='srvd-c_salesordermanage_sd-0001';va='com.sap.gateway.srvd.c_salesordermanage_sd.v0001.ae-c_salesordermanage.createwithsalesordertype.organizationdivision.SalesOrderManageType.X'/$metadata?sap-language=EN

However, when the request reaches the ICM, the raw HTTP request shows that:

  • = is encoded as %3D

  • %27 is converted back to '

Example from ICM level 3 trace:

GET /sap/opu/odata4/.../0001;ps%3D'srvd-c_salesordermanage_sd-0001';va%3D'com.sap.gateway.srvd.c_salesordermanage_sd.v0001.ae-c_salesordermanage.createwithsalesordertype.organizationdivision.SalesOrderManageType.X'/$metadata?sap-language=EN

The SAP Gateway expects the “=” characters to remain unencoded, and because of this encoding, the request fails.

Landscape and behavior

Middleware involved

  • SAP Web Dispatcher

  • Corporate HTTP Proxy

Observed behavior

  1. All apps work when the proxy is bypassed (Web Dispatcher active)

  2. All apps work when the Web Dispatcher is bypassed (proxy active)

  3. The issue occurs only when both proxy and Web Dispatcher are active

This strongly indicates that the URL is being modified due to an interaction between the proxy and Web Dispatcher.

Troubleshooting performed

  • All required roles, authorizations, services, and ICF nodes are active

  • Web Dispatcher profile parameters adjusted to prevent URL encoding

  • Web Dispatcher mod file changes attempted for OData V4 handling
    → Issue still persists

As per internal network team analysis:

  • A change may be required in the Web Dispatcher index file

  • Neither the network team nor BASIS team has access to modify it

  • Only SAP can make changes at this level

Questions to the community

  • Has anyone experienced a similar OData V4 URL encoding issue with Web Dispatcher + proxy?

  • Is this a known limitation or defect in SAP Web Dispatcher for OData V4?

  • Are there any supported parameters, SAP Notes, or workarounds to prevent encoding of = in the URL?

  • Should this be handled via ICM settings, Web Dispatcher configuration, or proxy rules?

  • Is there any recommended architectural workaround until SAP provides a fix?

Any guidance, experiences, or references would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support.

Varsha J S

 

#SAP #S4HANA #ODataV4 #SAPGateway #SAPWebDispatcher #ICM #SAPBasis #SAPFiori #SAPCommunity
@SAP @SAPCommunity @SAPSupport

entry.title: Analytical list Page Multiselection
entry.content:
Hello everyone.

I am currently working on a University Project and have the following problem.


I am working with S/4HANA and have developed an ALP using Fiori Elements (CDS, OData V2, on-premise, VS Code).
Using intent-based navigation, it is possible to transfer a single entry to another application where edits can be made.
My goal is to select multiple entries in the ALP table and transfer them to the next application List Report e.g for mass editing (based on their IDs).


Is this possible? Is there an alternative solution to realize this use case?


Thank you and kind regards

entry.title: Build Work Zone Returning 401
entry.content:
I have deployed an app on SAP BTP. The app has a service worker as it is a PWA and served through Build Work Zone Managed App Router. Everything works fine until the authentication cookie expires. Once the cookie expires, the app triggers reloading the index.html page, detects that the user is not logged in anymore and takes us to Build Work Zone login screen. In the authentication process, https://*.ondemand.com/login/callback?authType=ias&code
always gets 401 . If I remove the service worker , it works . Even if the service worker does not intercept any request, just it's presence is breaking the auth flow. 
Is there anyone who can help me figure out what's wrong here. Thanks for your time and suggestion.

entry.title: Fiori Migration to SAP BTP: Best Practice for Multi-Backend (Region-Based) Routing Without SAP Gatew
entry.content:
Hello community,

I am working on a Fiori migration project and would like guidance on the recommended architecture for handling multiple backend systems by region after moving to SAP BTP.

Current  Architecture

  • An on-premise SAP Gateway hosts a set of Fiori apps.

  • The Gateway forwards OData calls to multiple backend systems (ECC) based on system alias.

  • This setup allows region-based routing, but currently all backends are technically reachable, and multiple backends may be called during app execution.

Target Architecture

  • Decommission the on-premise SAP Gateway system.

  • Host Fiori apps on SAP BTP and expose them via SAP Build Work Zone.

  • Keep the same backend ECC systems (one per region).

  • No central Gateway system remains for system-alias-based routing.

Our Proposed Approach

  • Define one SAP BTP Destination per backend system (e.g. one per region).

  • Use the same destination names across subaccounts (DEV / QAS / PRD), with destination details varying per subaccount.

  • Configure the Fiori app to call all destinations and aggregate the results on the UI side.

     

Questions

  1. Is this approach (calling all backends and aggregating results in the UI) considered a good practice on SAP BTP?

  2. Are there recommended alternatives for region-based backend routing without a central SAP Gateway?

  3. How is user-region–based backend selection typically handled in BTP-centric Fiori architectures?

Any reference architectures or SAP-recommended patterns would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support.

entry.title: How PFCG / Fiori Content Manager finds OData services for catalogs
entry.content:
Hello all, I am triying to understand in detail how a sap fiori feature works. But I am not finding specific documentation for this.

The feature is the 'check services' button in SAP fiori content manager. This button lists the odata services related to the apps inclued in catalog. 

PFCG tcode shows the same list while adding catalogs.

The main question is: How exacly the system lists odata services for catalogs before add it to the role?

It is not clear yet how system select these odata services. The SAP Learning course 'Exploring the Authorization Concept for SAP Fiori on SAP S/4HANA' says in topic "Explaining SAP Fiori Authorization Model" says about authorization proposals in SU24.

My services contais authorization proposals, the app ID are correct in manifest.json and lauchpad designer target mapping tab for catalog.

entry.title: How to remove messagestrip from F3893 adapted fiori project
entry.content: How to remove messagestrip from F3893 adapted fiori project. There is no option to hide it from adapt ui

entry.title: Select the Most Appropriate Fiori Elements Floorplant (or Free Style)
entry.content:
Greetings, community experts.

I have the following scenario for developing a Fiori app using the CAP framework (NodeJS) on BTP.

The app needs to display various information about a Production Batch: general batch information, its list of items, and also allow the user to capture weights taken from an electronic scale connected to the system.

Initially, I'm leaning towards an Object Page, with its header and several sections. Perhaps even a custom section with Fiori Element extensions for weight capture.

My question stems from the following: How would the user reach such Object Page? Up until now, I've always used an Object Page as part of the List Report - Object Page pattern. However, in the scenario of this application I need to build, the user wouldn't reach the Object Page after searching for/selecting an item from a list. Instead, the user scans (with an electronic scanner) a label that has a barcode (or QR code), which contains the batch ID that needs to be displayed/processed. In other words, what should the homepage look like?

I've considered an object page with no content (but I'm not sure if that's possible) or a very simple freestyle page with an input field and a "Go" button or something similar (that activates on scan and navigates to the object page).

However, since I have several ideas but nothing concrete, I've decided to ask the community here. Is there a more appropriate pattern or approach for this application scenario?

Thanks in advance for any answers and/or suggestions.

entry.title: What are standard FIori application can be use in SAP ECC WM system
entry.content:
What are standard FIori application can be use in SAP ECC Warehouse management syetsm and which fiori application can be use for Pickling and packing process. 

What are common application that can be used in WM and EWM ?

entry.title: Delete F4579 configuration
entry.content:
We created a SAP Business Configuration via the ADT generator, usable in F4579.

We now want to delete it.

If we look up the maintenance object via Open Object in Eclipse, this gives an error.

We tried deleting it via the TR, and via TADIR. This does not work: the configuration is still there in F4579.

Does anyone know how to do this?

entry.title: Fiori "My Inbox" Error - Intermittent issue,
entry.content:
Hello,

I encounter an issue in fiori " My Inbox". The error is intermittent (sometimes there's an error but sometimes it is working properly)


sample uri with error(sorry but I cannot post the whole URI):    /sap/opu/odata/IWPGW/TASKPROCESSING;v=2;mo/TaskCollection/$count/?$filter=Status%20eq%20%27READY%........

Method: GET

Status code: 500 after successful login, 400 after clicking the My Inbox with error

 

If the tile has error , this error message will appear

I think there's no issue in the configuration because sometimes it is working

The RFC Destination is NONE, and connection Type is "I" (Internal Connection)

the ZTASKPROCESSING we maintained in /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE and the taskprocessing maintained in SICF is activated

no dump in ST22

no duplicate keys/task 

but there is an error in /IWFND/ERROR_LOG and IWBEP/ERROR_LOG

Frontend - An exception was raised

Backend - Soapfaultcode: Authentication failed (not sure yet if this is the root cause)

FYI: we are using BPM for the workflow, the standard software version we used in /IWFND/ROUTING is /IWPGW/BPM, current system is ECC. The tasks in the "My Inbox" is coming from custom table

what could be the other possible cause of the intermittent error, any idea or anyone has experienced this kind of issue? or can you share your techniques on how to find the root cause of error

entry.title: Code Page Conversion Error When Downloading UI5 BSP Apps in S/4HANA (CP1252 Issue)
entry.content:
Hello everyone, and thank you in advance for your support.

We previously worked with the 9.4 system version and used /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_LOAD to download UI5 BSP applications without any issues. Now that we are working on an S/4HANA system, we are facing a new problem during the download process.

When we try to download a BSP UI5 app, the report throws the following error:

Code
 
An code page conversion error occurred (path: controller/Worklist-dbg.controller.js / code page: Cp1252)
Warning: Text file ... has not been downloaded

In S/4HANA, the UI5 repository contains the minified .js files along with their .js.map source maps. During the download, the system appears to generate the corresponding *-dbg.js debug versions, and this step triggers a CP1252 code page conversion error, even though the system and SAP GUI are both Unicode. There is also no ZIP download option available in this S/4 environment.

Has anyone encountered this behavior in S/4HANA UI5 apps? Is there an SAP‑recommended workaround for downloading or modifying UI5 controllers when the system generates debug files that cannot be converted?

Thank you for any insights.

entry.title: Recommended alternatives to Report Painter for multi-step Income Statements in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am looking for the current best practice to replace Report Painter for financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA.

Specifically, I need to generate a multi-step Income Statement (Profit and Loss statement) that displays various profit levels in a specific order, such as:

  1. Revenue

  2. Cost of Sales

  3. Gross Profit (Subtotal 1 - 2)

  4. Selling, General and Administrative Expenses

  5. Operating Profit (Subtotal 3 - 4) ...and so on.

The Challenges:

  • Report Painter/Report Writer is considered maintenance mode (deprecated) in S/4HANA, so we want to move toward a more future-proof solution.

  • The standard Financial Statement Version (FSV) in Fiori/GUI typically starts with "Net Income/Loss" at the top of the hierarchy. Expanding the tree from the top-down does not meet our requirement for a traditional "Report Form" (step-by-step calculation) layout where Revenue comes first.

My Questions:

  • What is the recommended standard tool or architectural approach in S/4HANA to achieve this "Report Form" layout without heavy custom development?

  • Is there a way to define custom subtotals (like Operating Profit) that are not natively defined as a single node in the FSV hierarchy?

  • Should we look into Fiori-based analytical apps, or is an Excel-based integration (like Analysis for Office) the primary direction now?

I would appreciate any insights on the "SAP Standard" way to handle this.

Best regards,

entry.title: Issue with Updating Operation wise Confirmation Time via RAP Application
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

I am currently working on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud RAP application where we have implemented a custom field called “Execution Time” as per the business requirement.

The requirement is to update the execution time operation wise in the standard transaction CO03. For this purpose, we are using EML with I_ProductionOrderConfirmationTP to update the fields OpConfirmedWorkQuantity1 and OpConfirmedWorkQuantity2, which are intended to store the time values.

We have written the necessary logic to update both quantity and time using the same EML statement. However, the changes are not getting reflected as expected in the system.

For your reference, we have also attached a snapshot of the Time field along with the relevant code.

Could you please review and suggest if there is an alternative approach or any correction required to achieve the desired update?

Best Regards,

Thank you,

READ ENTITIES OF i_productionordconfirmationtp
ENTITY productionorderconfirmation
EXECUTE getconfproposal
FROM VALUE #(( confirmationgroup = lv_opconf
             %param-confirmationyieldquantity = <lfs_childbillet>-totalweight
            ) )
        RESULT DATA(lt_confproposal)
        REPORTED DATA(lt_reported_conf).

LOOP AT lt_confproposal ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_confproposal>).
APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_confirmation ASSIGNING FIELD SYMBOL(<ls_confirmation>).
       <ls_confirmation>-%cid = 'Conf' && sy-tabix.
       <ls_confirmation>-%data = CORRESPONDING #( <ls_confproposal>-%param ).
       <ls_confirmation>-%data-ConfirmedExecutionStartTime = lv_start_ts.
       <ls_confirmation>-%data-ConfirmedExecutionEndTime   = lv_end_ts.
       <ls_confirmation>-%data-finalconfirmationtype = 'X'.

 READ ENTITIES OF i_productionordconfirmationtp
      ENTITY productionorderconfirmation
      EXECUTE getgdsmvtproposal
FROM VALUE #( ( confirmationgroup = <ls_confproposal>-confirmationgroup
            %param-confirmationyieldquantity =  <lfs_childbillet>-totalweight
            ) )
      RESULT DATA(lt_gdsmvtproposal)
      REPORTED DATA(lt_reported_gdsmvt).
      CHECK lt_gdsmvtproposal[] IS NOT INITIAL.
      CLEAR lt_target[].
      DELETE lt_gdsmvtproposal[] WHERE %param-quantityinentryunit IS INITIAL.

        LOOP AT lt_gdsmvtproposal ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_gdsmvtproposal>)
        WHERE confirmationgroup = <ls_confproposal>-confirmationgroup .
        APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_target ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_target>).
       
        <ls_target> = CORRESPONDING #( <ls_gdsmvtproposal>-%param ).
        <ls_target>-%cid = 'Item' && sy-tabix.
        ENDLOOP.

  APPEND VALUE #( %cid_ref = <ls_confirmation>-%cid
                  %target = lt_target
  confirmationgroup = <ls_confproposal>-confirmationgroup ) TO lt_matldocitm.

      ENDLOOP.

      MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_productionordconfirmationtp
      ENTITY productionorderconfirmation
      CREATE FROM lt_confirmation
      CREATE BY \_prodnordconfmatldocitm FROM lt_matldocitm
      MAPPED DATA(poconfirmmapped)
      FAILED DATA(poconfirmfailed)
      REPORTED DATA(poconfirmreported).

    ENDIF.

entry.title: Develop custom report in SAP S/4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am currently working with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Version 2602.0.59 and would like to develop a custom report within the system. Could you please guide me on the required steps to achieve this?

Any insights, best practices, or resources on how to approach this task in SAP S/4HANA Cloud would be greatly appreciated.

Looking forward to your responses!

Best regards,
Harshita

entry.title: F3445 Maintain PIRs
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a few issues with Fiori App "F3445 Maintain PIRs".

1. When I download to spreadsheet, all values are being contained in first column A of the Excel and delimited by comma:

But in this blog it shows, that it is downloaded with values in different columns. Is this configurable?:

Creating Independent Requirements from a spreadsheet

2. I do not have button "Version Active":

I need like here:

Thanks.

entry.title: How to set a GroupHeaderListItem inactive in tabular suggestion
entry.content:
Hello community. I am struggling with the grouping function for the tabular suggestion of an Input. The basic grouping works just fine but the created row (GroupHeaderListItem) is always active and selectable which confuses users. I have tryed the 

"groupHeaderFactory" function that is called correctly but the table itself seems to ignore the "type" property of the GroupHeaderListItem. See the code below:
  private createGroupHeader(groupTitle: { key: string }): GroupHeaderListItem {
    return new GroupHeaderListItem({ title: groupTitle.key, type: 'Inactive' });
  }

I am on version 1.139.0.

Is this a bug or is there an other way to get the GroupHeader inactive? Thanks.

/BR Tobias

entry.title: How to create a Segment in Fiori?
entry.content:
Hello Community,

Can anybody help me by outlining the steps to create a segment for segment reporting in Fiori? I am able to accomplish this in GUI, but I also want to be able to do it on our Fiori system.

I would be extra grateful if someone could provide the following information, specifically:

-Necessary roles

-Necessary catalogs/apps

-Step-by-step guide on segment creation

(I know how to select a segment when creating profit center master data; I just need to know how to create a new segment.)
